q: Human eye consists of ___ organs which are capable of receiving visual images.
The human eye sends visual information to the ___.
The human eye is very similar to a ___ in many aspects.
The ___ opens and closes the eye, similar to a camera's lens cover.
The front portion of the eye is covered by a tough, transparent membrane called the ___.
The cornea is a tough, ___ membrane covering the front of the eye.
The region between the cornea and the eyelens is filled with a transparent liquid called the ___ humour.
The ___ humour is a transparent liquid found between the cornea and the eyelens.
The region between the eyelens and the retina contains a transparent liquid called the ___ humour.
The ___ humour is a transparent liquid found between the eyelens and the retina.
The ___ is the colored portion of the eye.
The iris controls the amount of ___ energy entering the eye.
The ___ is in the centre of the iris and the light passes through it.
The pupil is similar to the ___ in the camera.
The ___ adjusts the size of the pupil in the eye.
The pupil automatically ___ in dim light.
The pupil automatically ___ in bright light.
The ___ muscles are attached to the eyelens.
The ciliary muscles can vary the ___ of the eyelens.
Ciliary muscles alter the ___ of the lens to obtain a sharp image on the retina.
When near objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles ___ and permit the eyelens to form a more convex shape.
Viewing near objects causes the eyelens to form a more ___ shape.
When distant objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles ___ and the eyelens becomes less convex.
Viewing distant objects causes the eyelens to become ___ convex.
The ___ is the light sensitive area of the cells, at the back of the eye.
The retina consists of ___ and cones.
The ___ spot is the most light-sensitive spot on the retina.
The yellow spot on the retina is used for viewing very ___ objects.
The yellow spot consists of ___ exclusively.
There is a spot on the retina which has no ___ or cones.
When an image is formed at the blind spot it ___ be seen at all.
The spot where the optic nerve enters the eye is called the ___ spot.
The light energy falling on the retina is transmitted to the brain by means of the ___ nerve.
The cornea, the aqueous humour, the eyelens and the vitreous humour are ___ substances.
The image formed on the retina is ___ and inverted.
The image formed on the retina is real, ___ and smaller than the size of the object.
The image formed on the retina is smaller than the ___ of the object.
The common defects of vision are ___ (hyperopia) and nearsightedness (myopia).
___ is a defect of vision in which distant objects are seen clearly, but objects nearby are blurry.
In farsightedness, distant objects are seen clearly, but objects ___ are blurry.
___ is a defect of vision in which near objects are seen clearly, but objects far away are blurry.
In nearsightedness, near objects are seen clearly, but objects ___ away are blurry.
Defects of vision can be caused by ciliary muscles that cannot contract or relax ___.
Defects of vision can also be caused by wrongly shaped ___.
For farsightedness, rays from near objects intersect at a point ___ the retina.
Farsightedness can be due to an eyeball which is too ___.
Farsightedness can be due to an eyelens which cannot be sufficiently ___.
Farsightedness can be corrected by using spectacles with a ___ lens.
Farsightedness can be corrected by using spectacles with a ___ lens.
For nearsightedness, rays from distant objects intersect at a point in ___ of the retina.
Nearsightedness can be due to an eyeball which is too ___.
Nearsightedness can be due to an eyelens which is more ___ than is necessary.
Nearsightedness can be corrected by using spectacles with a ___ lens.
Nearsightedness can be corrected by using spectacles with a ___ lens.
People with normal vision can see an object in greatest ___ at a certain distance from the eye.
The least distance of distinct vision is about ___ cm.
People with normal vision can see clearly objects which are at ___.
For normal vision the maximum distance of distinct vision is ___.
When objects at infinity are viewed, the eyes are ___.
